Title: Fungal symbiont Mycena complements impaired nitrogen utilization in Gastrodia elata and supplies indole-3-acetic acid to facilitate its seed germination

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces nitrite to ammonia using reducing power from NAD(P)H in fungal nitrogen assimilation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Indole-3-acetic acid; nitrogen nutrients supporting seed germination
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Mycena hyphae and culture; functional effect observed in G. elata seeds/protocorms
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: IAA promotes seed germination and protocorm development; assimilated nitrogen supports tryptophan biosynthesis, energy metabolism, and growth.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes symbiotic germination of G. elata seeds by enabling fungal nitrite assimilation, supporting tryptophan and IAA production; deletion impairs nitrate utilization, lowers tryptophan/tryptamine and IAA, and reduces seed germination.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome profiling during symbiosis; Pearson correlation with germination rate; targeted gene deletion by homologous recombination; fungal growth assays on different nitrogen media; RT-qPCR; hormone/metabolite quantification; symbiotic germination assay; metabolomics; transmission electron microscopy
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step in nitrogen assimilation for the symbiotic effect
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated during symbiosis, especially at 10-30 days after co-culture; positively correlated with G. elata seed germination rate
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Mycena genome BioProject PRJNA1297343; interactive transcriptome BioProject PRJNA1303739; metabolomics by UHPLC-MS/MS accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Symbiotic co-culture with G. elata seeds at 25°C in darkness; nitrogen source variation in minimal medium
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion causes impaired fungal growth on nitrogen media and reduced substrate invasion/expansion on sawdust cornmeal medium.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful fungal metabolic engineering target to improve orchid symbiotic germination efficiency by enhancing nitrogen assimilation and downstream auxin supply; should be deployed while maintaining fungal growth vigor.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MyNir disruption also reduced IAA by 37.22%-38.57%, indicating nitrogen assimilation constrains tryptophan-dependent auxin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MyNir is induced during Mycena-G. elata symbiosis and positively correlates with seed germination rate. 2. MyNir deletion strongly impairs fungal nitrogen utilization and colonization ability. 3. Loss of MyNir decreases tryptophan, tryptamine, and IAA levels and blocks G. elata seed germination by disrupting hormone crosstalk, energy mobilization, and cellular restructuring.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes indole-3-acetamide to indole-3-acetic acid in the fungal tryptophan-dependent auxin biosynthesis pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Mycena hyphae and culture; functional effect observed in G. elata seeds/protocorms
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: IAA promotes symbiotic seed germination, protocorm development, hormone crosstalk, cell wall remodeling, and polysaccharide-to-monosaccharide conversion.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes G. elata seed germination by supplying fungal-derived IAA; deletion reduces IAA production and strongly inhibits symbiotic germination.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome profiling during symbiosis; correlation with seed swelling index; targeted gene deletion by homologous recombination; RT-qPCR; HPLC quantification of IAA; symbiotic germination assay; protocorm metabolomics; transmission electron microscopy
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step in the symbiotic IAA supply route
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated during symbiosis; amidase genes positively correlated with G. elata seed width-to-length ratio
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Mycena genome BioProject PRJNA1297343; interactive transcriptome BioProject PRJNA1303739; metabolomics by UHPLC-MS/MS accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Symbiotic co-culture with G. elata seeds at 25°C in darkness
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion reduces fungal IAA production and substrate invasion/expansion ability, leading to poor symbiotic performance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Priority fungal engineering target to improve orchid seed germination and protocorm formation through enhanced exogenous auxin delivery; could be combined with nitrogen assimilation genes such as MyNir.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Multiple amidase genes were present and correlated with germination, but EVM0010270 was chosen as the representative gene for functional validation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MyAmid is upregulated during symbiosis and its expression correlates with protocorm swelling. 2. MyAmid deletion reduces IAA production by 61.33%-69.28%. 3. Loss of MyAmid suppresses G. elata seed germination and alters auxin signaling, polysaccharide metabolism, and cellular ultrastructure in protocorms.
